title: omega fails on n^2 % 2 = n % 2 without a linear bridge
informal_statement: For every natural number n, n^2 and n have the same remainder mod 2 (the same parity): n ^ 2 % 2 = n % 2. A goal of this shape attacked directly by `omega` before the square is case-split or expanded into a linear form.
formal_statement_pp: theorem sq_mod_two_eq_self_mod_two : ∀ n : ℕ, n ^ 2 % 2 = n % 2

failure.input_context_summary: Goal `n ^ 2 % 2 = n % 2` over ℕ after `intro n`.
failure.attempt_summary: Applied `omega` directly to the goal. omega only reasons about linear integer/nat arithmetic, so it treats `n ^ 2` as an opaque atom unrelated to `n` (internally introducing fresh terms `c := n ^ 2`, `d := n ^ 2 / 2` with no linear link back to `a := n`, `b := n / 2`). It cannot derive that the parity of `n ^ 2` tracks the parity of `n`, and fails deterministically (kernel_fail / tactic_failure, ~4.6s) rather than proving or timing out.
failure.lesson: omega only handles linear arithmetic over ℤ/ℕ; a nonlinear term like `n ^ 2` becomes an opaque atom it cannot relate back to `n`. Prove parity-of-square goals by case-splitting on the parity of `n` first (e.g. `rcases Nat.even_or_odd n with ⟨k, rfl⟩ | ⟨k, rfl⟩`, then `ring_nf`/`omega` on the now-expanded linear form), or via a dedicated parity lemma — never a direct `omega` call on the unexpanded power.

notes: Negative example captured via a tracked proofsearch episode (problem_version 3ea68a09-d6cb-48e6-9b8e-b226d225268a, episode 7381250f-097a-4151-b5ba-0e80303ff42e, root_statement_hash f37a3f71285f910657be8cc6d69d66e8029587a40898f2a58662a3506fb19b71). Single `solve` attempt (`intro n; omega`) rejected with kernel_fail/tactic_failure; episode closed via `give_up`. unsafe_dev_attestation=true (dev/negative-example capture, not a benchmark or fidelity-reviewed problem) so this is capped at attested, never certified.
